prose:
Frontier-orbital energies for benzene were computed using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard') in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry: user-supplied .xyz file (single point, no re-optimization). SCF converged in 8 cycles. No warnings reported; all integrity checks passed (trustworthy: true).

Results:
- HOMO (index 20): −7.0468329 eV
- LUMO (index 21): −0.41574728 eV
- HOMO–LUMO gap: 6.6310857 eV (Koopmans-style orbital energy difference)
- Total electronic energy: −232.33724 Hartree (−6322.2183 eV)

The HOMO/HOMO−1 near-degeneracy (Δ = 0.0005 eV) and LUMO/LUMO+1 near-degeneracy (Δ = 0.0006 eV) are consistent with benzene's D₆h symmetry (degenerate e₁g HOMOs and e₂u LUMOs).

CLI invocation: /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --multiplicity 1 /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/benzene/benzene.xyz
